description:
You are an infrastructure engineer writing a tiny helper script that will later be used by a larger provisioning system.  
For now, create a one–off, **single-command** network-diagnostic that captures the current routing table.

REQUIREMENTS
1. Make sure the directory “/home/user/network_checks” exists.  
2. Execute exactly one shell command that prints the current routing table in the terse form (`ip route`).  
3. Redirect that exact, unaltered command output (no banners, prompts, comments, or extra lines) into the file  
   “/home/user/network_checks/routes.log”.  
4. The resulting file must  
   • exist at “/home/user/network_checks/routes.log”,  
   • be non-empty, and  
   • end with a single UNIX newline character (`\n`).  
5. Do not write anything else to stdout or to any other files; only the log file should be created/modified.

FORMAT THAT WILL BE AUTOMATICALLY TESTED
• Path: /home/user/network_checks/routes.log  
• The file must contain the literal output of `ip route`, exactly as the command prints it.  
• There must be at least one line; the first word of the first line must be “default”.  
• The very last byte of the file must be a newline character.

The automated checker will:
a) verify that “/home/user/network_checks/routes.log” exists,  
b) confirm it is not empty,  
c) read the first word of the first line (should be “default”), and  
d) verify the file’s final byte is `\n`.

Perform the task entirely within a single interactive terminal session.
truth:
Directory that must exist: /home/user/network_checks  
File that must be produced: /home/user/network_checks/routes.log  
Log-file requirements for grading:
  – file exists  
  – filesize > 0  
  – first token == "default"  
  – last byte == 0x0a

The grading harness will run its own `ip route` for content comparison if needed, so only the path and structural constraints are hard-coded. No pre-existing files or directories are provided before the task starts.
